steps.title: Identify what the question needs
steps.content: The question asks for the most logical transition. The first sentence states the geologic principle of cross-cutting relationships: an intrusion is younger than the rocks it cuts through. The second sentence applies that principle to a specific example — a geophysicist using it to date an igneous intrusion. The blank must signal that the second sentence follows as a direct logical consequence of the first.

steps.title: Eliminate the choices
steps.content:
• "Accordingly,": Signals that the second sentence follows logically from the first — the geophysicist's ability to date the intrusion is a direct consequence of the stated principle. ✓
  • "Moreover,": Adds information rather than showing a logical result. The second sentence is not adding a new point; it is applying the principle just stated. ✗
  • "To this end,": Signals purpose — implying the principle was stated in order to achieve the geophysicist's analysis. That reverses the logical direction. ✗
  • "That being said,": Signals contrast or concession, but there is no contrast between the two sentences. ✗
That matches the correct answer — A is correct.
